title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Go Client Systems · Runtime Recall
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for redirect semantics, method rewriting, credential scope, and loop control, producing auditable redirect policy engine. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http auditable redirect policy engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Resolve Location references against the current request URL and validate the resulting destination each hop. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Bound redirect hops, detect loops, retain the chain, and expose the policy decision to callers. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
